title: 10 best herbal teas for dry scalp
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dry scalp are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and roots that are known to soothe and moisturize a dry, itchy scalp. These teas are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help to nourish the scalp and promote healthy hair growth. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for dry scalp include chamomile, calendula, rosemary, and nettle. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce scalp irritation, while calendula tea is known for its soothing and healing effects on dry, flaky skin. Rosemary tea stimulates blood flow to the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing dryness, and nettle tea is rich in nutrients that can help to strengthen hair and improve scalp health. By using herbal teas as a natural remedy for dry scalp, individuals can experience relief from itching, flaking, and irritation, leading to improved scalp health, hair growth, and overall confidence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with dry scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can soothe an itchy, dry scalp and reduce redness and irritation. It also contains flavonoids, which are powerful antioxidants that can improve circulation and promote hair growth. Additionally, yarrow tea has been shown to regulate sebum production, which can help to balance the natural oils in the scalp and prevent dryness. Its anti-fungal and anti-bacterial properties can also help to eliminate any potential scalp infections that may be contributing to dryness. Drinking yarrow tea or using it as a rinse can provide relief and help to maintain a healthy scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of flavonoids, help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry scalp.
Astringent: The astringent properties of yarrow tea, caused by tannins, tighten skin and reduce flakiness, providing relief for dry scalp.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea's antimicrobial properties, from volatile oils like azulene, help maintain a healthy scalp by preventing the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of yarrow tea, due to phenolic acids, protect skin cells from damage, promoting overall scalp health and reducing dryness.
Circulation-boosting: Yarrow tea helps improve blood circulation, thanks to lactones, ensuring nutrients reach the scalp and promoting the growth of healthy hair follicles, alleviating dry scalp.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried yarrow flowers, simmering for 10-15 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the mixture steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the yarrow flowers, leaving you with the tea.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Consume the herbal yarrow tea daily to help alleviate dry scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tea helps with dry scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe an itchy, dry scalp and promote hair growth. Wormwood contains chemicals called sesquiterpene lactones, which can help stimulate blood flow to the scalp and provide essential nutrients to the hair follicles. Additionally, wormwood tea has high levels of antioxidants that can help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and irritation. Drinking wormwood tea or using it as a rinse can help moisturize and nourish the scalp, leaving it feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood tea can help reduce inflammation and irritation associated with dry scalp, thanks to compounds like sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help control the growth of bacteria and fungi that can worsen dry scalp conditions, due to the presence of active constituents like thujone, isothujone, and camphor.

Antioxidant: Wormwood tea contains antioxidants that protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall scalp health, with key antioxidants being flavonoids, phenolic acids, and rosmarinic acid.

Circulation-boosting: Wormwood tea can help improve blood flow to the scalp, ensuring proper nutrient supply and promoting hair growth, as a result of active constituents like camphor and 1,8-cineole.

Moisturizing: Wormwood tea has mild moisturizing properties that help soothe and hydrate dry scalp, providing relief from itching and flaking, due to the presence of polysaccharides and other water-soluble compounds.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried wormwood herb, then let it steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb and allow it to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Mix in 1-2 tablespoons of honey or lemon juice for added flavor and potential scalp benefits (optional).
Before bedtime, massage the tea into your dry scalp, making sure to cover all affected areas.
Leave the tea on your scalp for at least 30 minutes or overnight, then rinse it out thoroughly with warm water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with dry scalp because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that soothe and hydrate the scalp. The tea is rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which help to improve blood circulation and promote hair growth. It also has antiseptic and antifungal properties that can help to alleviate dry scalp conditions caused by dandruff or eczema. Regular use of pot marigold tea can help to maintain a healthy scalp and promote strong, shiny hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation caused by dry scalp, thanks to the active constituents like flavonoids and triterpenes.
Emollient: The emollient properties of pot marigold tea help soothe and soften dry, flaky skin on the scalp, due to the presence of plant sterols and fatty acids.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tea exhibits antimicrobial properties that can help control the growth of microorganisms contributing to dry scalp issues, as a result of active constituents such as essential oils and saponins.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in pot marigold tea helps protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage, which can worsen dry scalp conditions, owing to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.

Hydrating: Pot marigold tea promotes scalp hydration by improving skin barrier function, reducing water loss, and supporting skin cell regeneration, attributed to active constituents like mucilage and polysaccharides.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried pot marigold flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers, pouring the liquid into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink the tea daily to help soothe a dry scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with dry scalp because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an itchy, dry scalp. The tea's antioxidants, including epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dry scalp. White tea also contains vitamins B and E, which can help moisturize and nourish the scalp. Its natural antibacterial and antifungal properties can help maintain a healthy scalp by preventing the buildup of harmful bacteria and fungi. Regular use of white tea on the scalp can help improve scalp health, reduce dryness, and promote hair growth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce irritation and redness on the scalp, providing relief for dry, itchy skin. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and tannins.

Antioxidant: White tea contains a high level of antioxidants that help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy scalp environment. The high antioxidant content is attributed to high levels of catechins.

Antimicrobial: White tea exhibits antimicrobial properties that help maintain a balanced scalp microbiome, preventing the overgrowth of harmful bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate dry scalp conditions. This is due to the presence of polyphenols and caffeine.

Hydrating: White tea is naturally hydrating and can help moisturize the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness. This is attributed to its high levels of epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) and other catechins.

Anti-aging: White tea has anti-aging properties that can help improve skin elasticity and reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles on the scalp. This is due to the presence of EGCG and other polyphenols, which help protect collagen and elastin fibers in the skin.
remedy_recipe: Boil 4 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried chamomile flowers, letting it steep for 15 minutes.
In a separate pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 1 tablespoon of dried calendula flowers, steeping for 10 minutes.
Strain both mixtures and combine, allowing it to cool down to a warm temperature.
Stir in 2 teaspoons of lemon juice and 1 tablespoon of honey until fully dissolved.
Slowly pour the herbal tea over your scalp, massaging it gently, and let it sit for 20 minutes before rinsing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with dry scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that soothe and hydrate the scalp. The tea is rich in antioxidants and essential oils, such as azulene and bisabolol, which help to reduce inflammation, irritation, and itching. Additionally, chamomile tea can help to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dryness. The tea can be used as a rinse after shampooing or as a compress applied directly to the scalp. Regular use of chamomile tea can help to alleviate dry scalp and promote a healthy, moisturized scalp and hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, irritation, and swelling associated with dry scalp, due to the presence of α-bisabolol and apigenin.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help maintain a healthy scalp by preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi that can worsen dry scalp, attributed to the compound matricin.
Wound healing: German chamomile promotes wound healing, which can alleviate dry scalp symptoms, as it contains azulene and flavonoids that stimulate tissue regeneration.
Soothing: Its soothing properties help alleviate itchiness and discomfort associated with dry scalp, owing to the presence of α-bisabolol and apigenin, which have calming effects on skin.
Moisturizing: German chamomile tea has moisturizing properties that help hydrate and nourish the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness, due to its essential oils and antioxidants.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers.
Place the chamomile flowers in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the chamomile flowers.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea and let it cool down before using it as a rinse for your dry scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory tea helps with dry scalp because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can soothe and hydrate the scalp. Chicory root is rich in vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C, potassium, and magnesium, which can help to nourish and strengthen the hair follicles. Additionally, chicory tea has natural antifungal and antimicrobial properties that can help to combat scalp irritation and dandruff, which are often associated with dry scalp. By promoting scalp health and hydration, chicory tea can help to alleviate dry scalp symptoms and support overall hair health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chicory tea helps reduce inflammation, which is important for dry scalp as it can alleviate itching and irritation, and the key active constituents responsible are sesquiterpenes and polyphenols.
Moisturizing: Chicory tea has hydrating properties that help lock in moisture, preventing dryness, and this is due to the presence of mucilage, a type of complex carbohydrate.
Antioxidant: Chicory tea contains antioxidants that protect the scalp from damage and promote cell regeneration, and these antioxidants include various polyphenols and flavonoids.
Antibacterial: Chicory tea inhibits bacterial growth, preventing infections and promoting a healthy scalp, and the primary active constituents responsible are sesquiterpene lactones.
Soothing: Chicory tea has a calming effect on the skin, reducing redness and irritation, and this is due to the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 tablespoon of dried chicory root; let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the mixture steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the chicory root pieces.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Pour the tea into a glass and drink it daily for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with dry scalp because it is rich in silica and other minerals that can improve scalp health. Silica is known to promote collagen production, which can strengthen hair and improve its texture, while also soothing a dry, itchy scalp. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of horsetail tea may also help to reduce scalp irritation and inflammation, providing relief from dryness and discomfort. Furthermore, horsetail tea can help to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and providing additional nourishment to the hair follicles. These benefits make horsetail tea a popular natural remedy for dry scalp and other scalp-related issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea helps reduce inflammation, which is important for dry scalp as it can alleviate irritation and redness. The anti-inflammatory properties come from flavonoids and phenolic acids present in horsetail.

Antimicrobial: Horsetail tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, which is crucial for dry scalp as it can help prevent infections that may worsen the condition. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to the silica, phenolic acids, and flavonoids in horsetail.

Moisturizing: Horsetail tea provides hydration and helps lock in moisture, which is essential for dry scalp as it can help soothe and nourish the scalp. The moisturizing effect is due to the high silica content in horsetail.

Exfoliating: Horsetail tea aids in the removal of dead skin cells, which is vital for dry scalp as it promotes skin cell turnover and unclogs hair follicles. The exfoliating property is attributed to the silica and saponins present in horsetail.

Nutrient-rich: Horsetail tea is rich in minerals, such as silica, selenium, and manganese, which are important for dry scalp as they support healthy hair growth and improve scalp health. The nutrient-rich composition of horsetail helps strengthen hair, promote collagen production, and maintain overall scalp health.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Steep the horsetail herb in hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb and let it cool.
Drink 1 cup of the horsetail tea daily or use it as a rinse for your scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with dry scalp because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and nourish the scalp. The tea is rich in terpinen-4-ol, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation, which can help alleviate the symptoms of dry scalp. Additionally, the tea is high in antioxidants, which can help protect the scalp from damage and promote healthy cell growth. The tea can be used as a rinse or a compress to provide instant relief and hydration to a dry, itchy scalp. Regular use of Tasmanian blue gum tea can help maintain the scalp's natural moisture balance and promote a healthy, flake-free scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian blue gum tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation associated with dry scalp, thanks to its high content of eucalyptol and tannins.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help control the growth of bacteria and fungi that can worsen dry scalp conditions, due to the presence of compounds like terpinen-4-ol and α-pinene.
Moisturizing: Tasmanian blue gum tea provides hydration to the scalp, alleviating dryness and itchiness, as it contains essential oils and polyphenols with moisturizing properties.
Soothing: The soothing effects of the tea help calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort, owing to its rich supply of flavonoids and volatile oils.

Exfoliating: Its gentle exfoliating properties help remove dead skin cells and promote cell turnover, which can unclog hair follicles and improve overall scalp health, thanks to the presence of natural acids and saponins.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5-10 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried Tasmanian blue gum leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before gently massaging it onto your scalp. Leave it on for 10-15 minutes before rinsing it out.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blue felicia tea helps with dry scalp because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe an itchy, dry scalp. The tea is made from the flowers of the Blue Felicia plant, which contains high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation in the scalp. This increased blood flow can help to promote hair growth and nourish the scalp, keeping it hydrated and free from dryness. Additionally, the antioxidants in blue felicia tea can help to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can further contribute to dryness and irritation. By using blue felicia tea as a natural remedy, individuals can help to alleviate dry scalp and promote healthy, moisturized hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Blue felicia tea contains compounds that help reduce inflammation, which is important for dry scalp as it can alleviate irritation and redness. The active constituents responsible for this property include caffeine and flavonoids.

Moisturizing: Blue felicia tea has hydrating properties that help lock in moisture, making it ideal for dry scalp as it prevents further dryness and flaking. The primary active constituent responsible for this is theobromine.

Antioxidant: Blue felicia tea contains high levels of antioxidants that help protect the scalp from oxidative stress, promoting a healthy scalp environment. The active constituents responsible for this are polyphenols and catechins.

Antimicrobial: Blue felicia tea has antimicrobial properties that help maintain a balanced scalp microbiome, preventing the overgrowth of harmful bacteria that can exacerbate dry scalp. The active constituents responsible for this are caffeine and tannins.

Circulation-boosting: Blue felicia tea can improve blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dryness. The active constituents responsible for this are caffeine and theophylline.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 tablespoons of dried Herbal Blue Felicia leaves, bringing them to a boil.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat and strain the liquid to remove the leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Massage the tea into your scalp, allowing it to sit for 10-15 minutes before rinsing it out.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tea helps with dry scalp because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe and nourish the scalp. The tea is rich in vitamins and minerals that can help to moisturize and strengthen the hair, reducing dryness and flakiness. Additionally, the tea has been shown to improve blood circulation, which can promote hair growth and help to alleviate dry scalp conditions. Drinking maidenhair tree tea or using it as a rinse can provide relief from dry scalp and leave hair feeling soft, smooth, and healthy.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of maidenhair tree tea can help reduce inflammation and irritation associated with dry scalp, thanks to the presence of triterpenes and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in maidenhair tree tea helps protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage, promoting a healthy scalp environment, due to the action of catechins and other polyphenols.

Moisturizing: Maidenhair tree tea has moisturizing properties that help maintain the scalp's natural moisture balance, preventing dryness and flaking, attributed to the presence of polysaccharides and mucilage.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of maidenhair tree tea can help control the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi on the scalp, reducing the risk of infections and dandruff, owing to the presence of essential oils and tannins.

Circulation promotion: Maidenhair tree tea can improve blood circulation in the scalp, ensuring proper nutrient supply to hair follicles and promoting healthy hair growth, attributed to the presence of caffeic acid and other phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried maidenhair tree leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down.
Massage the tea onto your dry scalp, leaving it on for 10-15 minutes before rinsing.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry scalp with herbal teas is by rinsing your hair with cooled tea. Chamomile and calendula teas are ideal options due to their anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritation. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, helps maintain a healthy scalp. After shampooing, pour the cooled tea over your scalp, gently massaging it in. Allow the tea to sit for a few minutes before rinsing it out. Repeat this process once or twice a week for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry scalp that are treated with herbal teas include dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis. Chamomile tea is often used for its anti-inflammatory properties to soothe an itchy scalp, while green tea has antioxidants that can promote hair growth and reduce dandruff. Nettle tea is rich in vitamins and minerals that can help to strengthen hair and reduce scalp inflammation. Lavender tea is known for its calming and soothing effects, making it a popular choice for treating dry, itchy scalp. It is important to note that while herbal teas can provide relief for some scalp conditions, it is always best to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
